$comment: This file MUST be placed at: META-INF/extension.json inside your JAR.
2. Replace ALL values with your extension's details
4. Add to JAR: jar uf my-extension.jar META-INF/extension.json
name: Aniyomi: My Anime Extension
$comment_name: Human-readable name. Prefix with 'Aniyomi: ' (the app strips this).
$comment_pkgName: Unique Java package name. Also used as the JAR filename (pkgName.jar).
$comment_versionName: SemVer-like string. The part before the last '.' is the libVersion. Example: '15.4' → libVersion = 15.0
$comment_versionCode: Monotonically increasing integer. Higher = newer. Used for update detection.
$comment_libVersion: Minimum Aniyomi extensions-lib version required. Parsed from versionName. Currently supported: 12.0 – 15.0.
$comment_lang: ISO 639-1 language code of the source content. Used for filtering. Can be empty string if the source handles multiple languages.
$comment_isNsfw: Whether this extension contains adult/NSFW content. true → hidden unless user enables NSFW sources in Preferences.
$comment_isTorrent: Whether this extension provides torrent-based sources. true → shows torrent-specific UI elements.
$comment_sourceClass: Semicolon-delimited list of fully qualified source class names. Each class must implement eu.kanade.tachiyomi.source.Source, eu.kanade.tachiyomi.source.SourceFactory, or eu.kanade.tachiyomi.animesource.AnimeSource. If a class name starts with '.', it is resolved relative to pkgName (e.g., '.MySource' → 'com.example.animeextension.MySource').
$comment_pkgFactory: Optional. Fully qualified class name of a SourceFactory. If set, this factory's createSources() is called to create source instances. Can be null if sources are created directly from sourceClass entries.
$comment_hasReadme: Whether the extension includes a README or documentation. Displayed in the extension details UI.
$comment_hasChangelog: Whether the extension includes a changelog. Displayed in the extension update UI.
